name: Manorathanandin
dates_notes: Manorathanandin is conventionally placed in the ninth to tenth century as a later commentator on Dharmakīrti's Pramāṇa-Vārttika; exact dates are uncertain.
core_thesis: Manorathanandin is a Buddhist pramāṇa commentator known for his *Pramāṇavārttika-vṛtti* on Dharmakīrti. His importance is textual and exegetical: the work is one of the principal Sanskrit commentaries through which Dharmakīrti's difficult verses are read, arranged, and debated. It does not replace Dharmakīrti's system with a new one; it clarifies the inherited apparatus of perception, inference, apoha, *svabhāva-pratibandha*, and the Buddha-as-authority argument. The roster report marks availability as scan rather than clean etext, so this entry records the attested text but does not claim a local source.

engaged_works.title: Pramanavarttika-vrtti
engaged_works.composition_dates_notes: Composed in the later Indian commentary tradition on Dharmakīrti.
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely attributed to Manorathanandin in the Buddhist pramāṇa commentarial tradition.

ascription_evidence.description: Buddhist pramāṇa scholastic tradition transmits the commentary under Manorathanandin's name.

engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit commentary on Dharmakīrti's *Pramāṇa-Vārttika*. It explains the structure and meaning of Dharmakīrti's verses across the major topics of Buddhist epistemology: perception, inference, apoha, the analysis of the inferential mark, and the establishment of reliable authority. Its role in the corpus is exegetical and preservative, making it a necessary comparator for any serious treatment of Dharmakīrti's reception.
